source.page: no live /brands/day14-realty landing yet (realty killswitch active; admin-only at /admin/realty)

note: Realty is paused by ops killswitch and has no public landing on disk. These variants are first-pass conversion-focused options for when the page lands — anchored to the actual scoring engine + SW Florida county coverage so they can survive contact with the operator voice.
brandVoice: Operator voice — real records, not vibes.
Specific to SW Florida county feeds (Collier, Lee, Charlotte, Sarasota, Manatee).
Lean on the hourly scout + transparent deal score, not on broker theatre.
Anti-MLS-flexing, anti-'find your dream home', pro-actual-deal-flow.

variants.h1: Every SW Florida parcel, scored hourly, ranked the way investors actually buy.
variants.subhead: Day14 Realty pulls fresh records and AVM rents from Collier, Lee, Charlotte, Sarasota, and Manatee — then ranks every parcel against a transparent deal score, every hour. No MLS, no junk leads, no waiting for the next email blast.
variants.rationale: Conversion-tuned: the H1 names the geography + cadence + ranking model in one beat (the three things an investor visitor is filtering for). Subhead delivers the proof (counties, sources, transparent score) and ends on three anti-positions that mirror Day14's anti-SaaS / anti-agency core voice. Highest expected lift for the 'I want deal flow, not a portal' investor.

variants.h1: Deal flow that reads the county records, not the listing photos.
variants.subhead: An hourly scout ingests every SW Florida parcel feed, runs a credentialed deal score, and surfaces only the ones that pencil at today's rents. You get a ranked queue. You don't get the MLS's leftovers.
variants.rationale: Opens with a sharp anti-MLS hook the target audience already believes — converts the 'I'm tired of Zillow' investor segment. Closes the subhead with a benefit-statement ('ranked queue, not leftovers') instead of a feature list, which traditionally outperforms feature-stacked subheads for off-platform real-estate audiences.

variants.h1: Five counties. Every parcel. One transparent deal score, refreshed hourly.
variants.subhead: Day14 Realty turns the public record into a ranked buy list — county data in, scored opportunities out, every hour. Built by one operator who actually runs the numbers, not a SaaS dashboard that just visualizes the MLS.
variants.rationale: Cadenced numeric H1 (5 / every / 1 / hourly) reads like a stat strip — proven format for investor conversion. Subhead anchors the value chain ('public record in → scored opps out') in language that's easier to forward to a partner than abstract pitch lines. The 'one operator' close echoes Day14's core trust signal.
